page: Microsoft Azure DC32ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCADCCV5 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 1229 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the DC32ads_cc_v5 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2728.55, placing it in the top 25%. Its PassMark memory score of 2854.6 is also in the top tier. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 43210.72 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13612.0. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 69.65 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 46.54 GB/sec for large-block write operations. The server handles small-model LLM text generation efficiently, achieving 278.86 tokens/sec on a 135M model, though performance drops significantly to a weak 6.57 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a balanced resource density of vCPU to memory, making it a viable option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources and local storage without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The primary tradeoff is the lack of hardware acceleration, which limits its utility for large-scale machine learning tasks, as evidenced by the weak large-model LLM inference benchmarks. This server is suited for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from local storage and solid single-core execution.
description: Microsoft Azure DC32ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of RAM. It features a 1229 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage, but lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and high memory scores, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. While it handles small-model LLM text generation efficiently, performance degrades significantly on larger models. Qualitatively, the instance provides a balanced resource-to-compute profile, making it a cost-effective choice for workloads that do not require specialized hardware. It is suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC32ads_cc_v5 is a 32-vCPU general-purpose server with an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 128 GB RAM, and a 1229 GB local disk, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC32ads_cc_v5 is a 32-vCPU general-purpose server featuring an AMD EPYC processor, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, substantial local storage, and strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
128 GB of system memory paired with a 1229 GB local disk
Strong single-core CPU and memory performance in benchmark testing
Average multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth
Suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
Inadequate for large-scale LLM inference due to lack of GPU acceleration
